faq.question: Why does spacing matter?
faq.answer: Proper spacing ensures adequate sunlight, airflow, and nutrient access for each plant.

faq.question: Can I plant closer in raised beds?
faq.answer: Yes, raised beds with good soil can often use spacing 25% tighter than ground planting.

faq.question: Is the Garden Spacing Calculator free to use?
faq.answer: Yes, completely free with no signup required. Use it as many times as you need — there are no limits or hidden fees.

faq.question: How accurate is this calculator?
faq.answer: This calculator uses standard gardening and horticulture formulas trusted by gardeners and growers. Results are reliable estimates for planning purposes. For critical decisions, we recommend consulting a qualified professional to verify.

faq.question: What bed length should I enter?
faq.answer: Enter the most accurate bed length value you have available. If you're estimating, use a conservative figure. You can always run the calculator again with different values to see how changes affect the results.

sections.title: What Is the Garden Spacing Calculator?
sections.body: The Garden Spacing Calculator is a free online tool designed for gardeners and growers who need quick, accurate calculations in the gardening and horticulture space. By entering your bed length, bed width, plant spacing, you get instant results including number of rows, plants per row, total plants. No formulas to memorize, no spreadsheets to build — just enter your numbers and get the answer in seconds. Whether you're a beginner or experienced professional, this calculator saves you time and eliminates guesswork.

sections.title: Why This Calculation Matters
sections.body: Getting number of rows right can make the difference between success and costly mistakes. In gardening and horticulture, small errors compound quickly. Manual calculations are error-prone and time-consuming, especially under pressure. This calculator applies proven formulas used by gardeners and growers worldwide, giving you confidence that your numbers are correct. Use it to grow a thriving garden with precision and avoid common pitfalls that trip up beginners.

sections.title: When Should You Use This Calculator?
sections.body: This tool is most useful when you know your bed length and need to find the right number of rows. It's also great for quick estimates before committing to a decision, and to double-check manual calculations or professional quotes, and when comparing different scenarios side by side. Bookmark this page and come back whenever you need a fast, reliable answer — the calculator is always free and requires no signup.

howToSteps.title: Enter Your Bed Length (ft)
howToSteps.description: Type or select your bed length in the field provided. Use the most accurate value available for best results.

howToSteps.title: Enter Your Bed Width (ft)
howToSteps.description: Type or select your bed width in the field provided. Use the most accurate value available for best results.

howToSteps.title: Enter Your Plant Spacing (inches)
howToSteps.description: Type or select your plant spacing in the field provided. Use the most accurate value available for best results.

howToSteps.title: Click Calculate
howToSteps.description: Hit the Calculate button to run the numbers. Results appear instantly below.

howToSteps.title: Review Your Results
howToSteps.description: Check your number of rows, plants per row, total plants. Use these figures to inform your next decision or compare against alternative scenarios.

tips: Double-check your bed length before calculating — even small input errors can significantly change your results.
Run the calculator with different values to compare scenarios and find the optimal approach for your situation.
Pay attention to both number of rows and plants per row — they work together to give you the full picture.
Bookmark this page for quick access next time you need to grow a thriving garden.
If you're unsure about your plant spacing, start with a conservative estimate and adjust from there.
